rulings.Case Title: BRIAN MACGREGOR ET AL VS. SUNSHINE TRANSPORTATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To His Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two, From Forward Air
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for October 7, 2025, line 1, PLAINTIFF BRIAN MACGREGOR, CAREY SUCKOW Motion To Compel Further Responses To His Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two, From Forward Air Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to His Requests for Production of Documents, Set Two, From Forward Air, Inc. is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL CAREY JR. VS. HOTEL 242, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for October 7, 2025, line 2, DEFENDANT PARAMOUNT HOTELS, INC. Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition Defendant Paramount Hotels, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition is GRANTED. Moving Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.450. Subdivision (a) of that section provides: "If, after service of a deposition notice, a party to the action or an officer, director, managing agent, or employee of a party, or a person designated by an organization that is a party under Section 2025.230, without having served a valid objection under Section 2025.410, fails to appear for examination, or to proceed with it, or to produce for inspection any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice, the party giving the notice may move for an order compelling the deponent's attendance and testimony, and the production for inspection of any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice." Moving Defendant served Plaintiff Paul Carey with a deposition notice and Plaintiff (repeatedly) failed to appear or otherwise proceed with the deposition. Plaintiff's failure to appear or proceed is not excused. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition consistent with the terms set out in the notice on a date time agreed to by Plaintiff and Moving Defendant before November 4, 2025; absent agreement Plaintiff must appear for deposition on November 4, 2025, at 10:00 am at the address identified in the notice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JULIANA LOURENCO LOPES DEMASI VS. GOLDEN GATE OBSTETRICS AND GYNECOLOGY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Compensation Regarding Medical Negligence And Malpractice To The Minor Child Harmed By The Defendant?S Conduct; Opposition To Special Appearances
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for October 7, 2025, line 4, PLAINTIFF JULIANA LOPES DEMASI Motion For Compensation Regarding Medical Negligence And Malpractice To The Minor Child Harmed By The Defendant?S Conduct; Opposition To Special Appearances Plaintiff Juliana Lourenco Lopes Demasi's Motion For Compensation Regarding Medical Negligence And Malpractice To The Minor Child Harmed By The Defendant's Conduct; Opposition To Special Appearances is DENIED. No good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion is denied. This motion is Plaintiff's seventh meritless motion seeking substantially identical relief. Plaintiff is ordered to review her previous motions, the rulings on those motions and Code of Civil Procedure sections 391 through 391.7, paying particular attention to section 391, subdivision (b)(3). Plaintiff's objection to Defendant Regents' special appearance is overruled. Specially appearing Defendant Regents' invitation for the court on its own to move for sanctions is declined. Specially appearing Defendant is asked to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: DEUTSCHE BANK TRUST COMPANY AMERICAS, AS TRUSTEE, VS. 46 GEARY, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Approving Final Report And Accounting, Discharging Receiver, Exonerating Receiver'S Bond, And Terminating Receivership
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for October 7, 2025, line 5, OTHER MARK BUTCHER (RECEIVER FROM TRANSWESTERN REAL ESTATE SERVICES) Motion For An Order Approving Final Report And Accounting, Discharging Receiver, Exonerating Receiver'S Bond, And Terminating Receivership Plaintiff Deutsche Bank Trust Company of America's unopposed Motion For An Order Approving Final Report And Accounting, Discharging Receiver, Exonerating Receiver's Bond, And Terminating Receivership GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART. Good cause appearing, the court orders as follows: (1) The Final Report and Account is approved. All actions taken, contracts made, debts incurred or other transactions entered into by the Receiver shall be and hereby are ratified and approved; (2) That all accounts provided by the Receiver, including the Final Report and Account filed herein, are approved; (3) That the payment of fees and reimbursements of expenses due to the Receiver and his counsel, as set forth in the Final Report and Account, are approved; (4) All bonds, including, the Receiver's and Plaintiff's bonds are hereby exonerated; (5) That the Receiver and his counsel are released from liability for good faith actions taken consistent with the court orders arising out of or in connection with the receivership estate and the real property commonly known as 46-52 Geary Street and 45-47 Maiden Lane, San Francisco, California; (6) That following entry of this Order, the Receiver is authorized to pay, as funds become available, the unpaid fees and expenses of the Receiver and his counsel including all fees and expenses reflected in an Addendum to Final Report and Account to be filed by the Receiver within (60) sixty days after entry of this Order (any funds in Receiver's possession after these payments are made may be returned to Plaintiff, the special servicer, or to any person/entity that Plaintiff directs); and (7) That the receivership is terminated and the Receiver is hereby discharged of all duties, liabilities or obligations related to this action. Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. OCEANIC INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for October 7, 2025, line 9 PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment Plaintiff Gabriel Espinoza's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted. Based on the record before it, the court finds: (1) the injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Proposition 65; (2) the penalty amounts are reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2); and, (3) the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. The consent judgments shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the parties' [Proposed] Consent Judgment. Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the text above verbatim. Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a copy of the proposed consent judgment executed by all relevant parties. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE GUIRAL VS. LYFT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for October 7, 2025, line 11, DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay is DENIED. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
